explanations.4: This calls a function _d_check_env to verify that both 'fzf' and 'docker' commands are available on your system; if either is missing, the script stops and returns an error.
explanations.31: head -n 1 extracts just the first line from fzf_out, which contains the special key you pressed (like ctrl-r); if you didn't press a special key, this will be empty.
explanations.32: tail -n +2 means 'show me everything starting from line 2 onward', so this grabs the actual selected Docker image info minus the key that was pressed.
explanations.42: This builds a shell command that tries to find zsh first, falls back to bash if zsh doesn't exist, and finally uses sh as a last resort—ensuring the container starts with whatever shell is available.
explanations.43: tr -s ' ' squeezes multiple spaces into single spaces, cleaning up the command string so it looks neat before being executed.
